name: Cetirizine
evidence_notes: First-line MCAS rescue; reduces histamine-driven urticaria, pruritus, rhinorrhea.
source_refs: Afrin LB et al. 2020 MCAS consensus-2

name: Loratadine
evidence_notes: Non-sedating alternative; common in MCAS step-1 protocols.

name: Fexofenadine
evidence_notes: Often used at 2-4x standard dose in MCAS per consensus.

name: Hydroxyzine
evidence_notes: Used for sleep + neuro symptoms in MCAS.

name: Diphenhydramine
mechanism: H1 antagonist; rescue route for anaphylaxis
evidence_notes: Acute rescue; sedating.

name: Famotidine
evidence_notes: Standard with H1 in MCAS; GI + systemic histamine.

name: Ranitidine
evidence_notes: Withdrawn 2020 (NDMA impurity). Included for SAR/reference only.

name: Cromolyn sodium
mechanism: Inhibits mast cell degranulation; reduces Ca2+ influx
evidence_notes: Oral (Gastrocrom) for GI MCAS; poorly absorbed systemically.

name: Ketotifen
evidence_notes: Off-label in US; widely used by MCAS clinicians.

name: Ascorbic acid
mechanism: Accelerates histamine degradation; antioxidant; cofactor for DAO
evidence_notes: Adjunct; supports DAO activity.

name: Quercetin
mechanism: Inhibits PKC, Ca2+ influx, histamine + tryptase + cytokine release
evidence_notes: Outperforms cromolyn in human mast cell assays for some readouts.
source_refs: Weng Z et al. 2012 PLOS ONE; Theoharides 2015

name: Bromelain
mechanism: Reduces allergic inflammation; possible histamine modulation
evidence_notes: Adjunct in allergic rhinitis; mechanism not fully resolved.

name: Stinging nettle leaf
mechanism: Natural H1 antagonism + COX/LOX inhibition; multi-component
evidence_notes: Standardized extracts (Urtica dioica) studied in allergic rhinitis.

name: Diamine oxidase (DAO)
mechanism: Degrades dietary histamine in the gut lumen pre-absorption
evidence_notes: Taken before high-histamine meals; useful in histamine intolerance subset.

name: Montelukast
evidence_notes: Standard MCAS maintenance; respiratory + GI symptoms.

name: Naltrexone (low-dose)
mechanism: TLR4 antagonism; glial / immune modulation at low dose
evidence_notes: Anecdotal in MCAS, fibromyalgia, autoimmune; off-label.

name: Luteolin
mechanism: Inhibits PKC, NF-kB, mast cell + microglia activation; crosses BBB
evidence_notes: Stronger than quercetin in several neuro-mast-cell assays.

name: Curcumin
evidence_notes: Phytosomal forms (Meriva) improve bioavailability.

name: EGCG
mechanism: Mast cell stabilization; antioxidant; Nrf2 activator
evidence_notes: Polyphenol with broad anti-allergic effects.

name: Resveratrol
evidence_notes: Inhibits non-IgE pseudo-allergic activation.

name: Silibinin
evidence_notes: Active component of silymarin complex.

name: Apigenin
evidence_notes: Synergizes with quercetin/luteolin.

name: Kaempferol
evidence_notes: Common dietary flavonoid; broad signaling effects.

name: Hesperidin
evidence_notes: Studied in allergic rhinitis.

name: Rutin
evidence_notes: Quercetin-3-rutinoside; lower bioavailability.

name: Myricitrin
evidence_notes: Less-studied flavonoid worth SAR exploration.

name: Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3)
mechanism: Immunomodulation; mast cell stability; T-reg support
evidence_notes: Deficiency common in MCAS; correction reduces flare frequency.

name: Calcitriol
mechanism: VDR agonist; downregulates mast cell IgE response
evidence_notes: Active hormone; clinical use mainly in renal disease.

name: Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA)
evidence_notes: Reduces systemic inflammation; supports membrane stability.

name: Eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA)
mechanism: SPM precursor; reduces LTB4; mast cell modulation
evidence_notes: Co-administered with DHA in fish oil.

name: Butyric acid
mechanism: HDAC inhibition; gut barrier reinforcement; Treg induction
evidence_notes: Microbiome-derived; supports gut-mast axis.

name: N-Acetylcysteine (NAC)
mechanism: GSH replenishment; ROS scavenging; Nrf2 activation (indirect)
evidence_notes: Supports oxidative-stress reduction upstream.

name: Taurine
evidence_notes: Down-regulates degranulation in some assays.

name: Alpha-lipoic acid
evidence_notes: Neuroprotective; mitochondrial support.

name: Biotin
mechanism: Cofactor for carboxylases; downregulates degranulation (animal data)
evidence_notes: Adjunct only; weak direct evidence.

name: Baicalein
evidence_notes: Strong inhibition of histamine + leukotriene release.

name: Rosmarinic acid
evidence_notes: Studied in seasonal allergy trials.

name: Thymoquinone
evidence_notes: Active component of black cumin seed oil.

name: 6-Gingerol
mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; prostaglandin inhibition; mast cell stability
evidence_notes: Common GI-supportive natural.

name: Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid)
evidence_notes: Used in prostaglandin-high MCAS subset under clinical supervision (NSAID-tolerant only).

name: Masitinib
evidence_notes: Phase 3 trials for severe indolent systemic mastocytosis / severe MCAS.

name: Sulforaphane
mechanism: Nrf2 master activator; HO-1 induction; NF-kB / caspase-1 inhibition; epigenetic (DNMT/HDAC) modulation
evidence_notes: Upstream rescue candidate for oxidative + epigenetic 'injury'; suppresses mast cell degranulation in vitro.

name: Glucoraphanin
mechanism: Converted to sulforaphane by myrosinase; same downstream Nrf2 activation
evidence_notes: Bioavailability depends on co-administered myrosinase or gut bacteria.

name: Semaglutide
mechanism: GLP-1R agonism; broad anti-inflammatory; possible direct mast cell stabilization
evidence_notes: 2025 refractory-MCAS case series: ~89% response. Peptide — no canonical small-molecule SMILES.

name: Tirzepatide
mechanism: Dual GLP-1R + GIPR agonism; anti-inflammatory; metabolic stabilization
evidence_notes: Same 2025 case series cohort. Peptide.

name: Omalizumab
mechanism: Binds free IgE; downregulates FcεRI surface expression on mast cells/basophils
evidence_notes: Approved for chronic spontaneous urticaria; off-label in MCAS. Antibody — no SMILES.

name: Avapritinib
mechanism: Selective KIT D816V tyrosine kinase inhibition
evidence_notes: FDA-approved for advanced systemic mastocytosis (2021).

name: Midostaurin
evidence_notes: Approved for advanced systemic mastocytosis.
